title: populus × canadensis var. serotina
intro: Populus × canadensis var. serotina, also known as black cottonwood, is a hybrid of the native American white poplar (Populus alba) and the Canadian poplar (Populus grandidentata), and is a deciduous tree native to North America. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of black cottonwood include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as a natural expectorant to relieve congestion. The herbal preparations of black cottonwood include teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the bark in hot water or a solvent, and can be used to treat various health conditions.
